leadText: If you were looking for the item that Spiritomb is bound to, see Odd Keystone. A Key Stone (Japanese: キーストーン Key Stone) is a Key Item introduced in Generation VI, which is used to allow a Pokémon holding a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve. It is unobtainable in Generation VIII. When the Key Stone is activated, the Pokémon's corresponding Mega Stone reacts to it, starting the Mega Evolution process. Trainers often keep their Key Stone inside a personal accessory, such as a Mega Ring, Mega Bracelet, Z-Ring, or Z-Power Ring, though there are exceptions to this.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Key Stone allows the player to Mega Evolve their Pokémon if they hold the appropriate Mega Stone . A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment ZA ).

sections.title: Acquisition
sections.text: Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method X Y Mega Ring : Tower of Mastery (from Korrina after earning the Rumble Badge ) OR AS Mega Bracelet : Southern Island (from Steven after obtaining Latios OR / Latias AS ) S M Key Stone : Ancient Poni Path (from Dexio after becoming Champion ) US UM Key Stone : Poni Plains (from Dexio after becoming Champion ) P E Key Stone : Professor Oak's Laboratory (from Blue after earning seven Badges ) ZA Mega Ring : Jaune Sector 10 (from AZ before facing the Rogue Mega Absol )

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Mega Ring Games Description X Y This ring contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle. OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA This ring contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying Mega Stones to Mega Evolve in battle. S V ZA This ring contains an unknown power that somehow enables a Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle. Mega Bracelet Games Description OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA This cuff contains an untold power that somehow enables Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle. S V This bracelet contains an unknown power that somehow enables a Pokémon carrying a Mega Stone to Mega Evolve in battle. Key Stone Games Description OR AS A stone of unexplained power that makes Pokémon Mega Evolve. S M US UM A stone filled with an unexplained power. It makes Pokémon that battle with a Mega Stone Mega Evolve. P E A stone filled with an unexplained power. It reacts to Mega Stones and makes Pokémon Mega Evolve. Sw Sh BD SP LA S V A stone filled with an unexplained power. It makes Pokémon that battle with a Mega Stone Mega Evolve.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 History 1.2 Price 1.3 Effect 1.4 Description 1.5 Acquisition 1.6 In-game appearances 1.6.1 Player characters 1.6.2 Non-player characters 1.6.2.1 Pokémon X and Y 1.6.2.2 Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire 1.6.2.3 Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon 1.6.2.4 Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! 1.6.2.5 Pokémon Legends: Z-A 1.7 Gallery 1.7.1 Artwork 1.7.2 Sprites In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] History [ edit | edit source ] Despite being integral to the Mega Evolution phenomenon, little is known about the origin of Key Stones. In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire , Key Stones, along with Mega Stones , seem to have a strong connection to the meteoroids that rained down on the Hoenn region millenia ago. Matt and Professor Cozmo found a Key Stone and a Galladite , respectively, in Meteor Falls , an area known for having been struck by meteors in the distant past. [ 1 ] According to the lore passed down by the Draconids , as told by Zinnia at Sky Pillar , a huge, brilliant meteoroid that struck the planet 1,000 years ago, creating the crater where currently lies Sootopolis City , allowed for Rayquaza to respond to the people's wishes for salvation during a time of desperate need and undergo the world's first-ever Mega Evolution—in this sense, the meteoroid acted as a Key Stone. Maxie OR / Archie AS also considers the possibility of altering the Meteorite's qualities to those of a Key or Mega Stone. Unlike Mega Stones, Key Stones have not been associated with the firing of the ultimate weapon in Kalos . An old legend associated with the Tower of Mastery speaks of a Trainer and Lucario finding the first known Key Stone and Mega Stone in the area. [ 2 ] According to a loading screen text in Pokémon Legends: Z-A , Key Stones work by amplifying the bonds between Trainer and Pokémon, enabling them to channel the power of Mega Evolution. Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price X Y OR AS S M US UM P E N/A N/A Effect [ edit | edit source ] The Key Stone allows the player to Mega Evolve their Pokémon if they hold the appropriate Mega Stone . A Trainer can only Mega Evolve one Pokémon per battle (or only have one Mega-Evolved Pokémon at any given moment ZA ). Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method X Y Mega Ring : Tower of Mastery (from Korrina after earning the Rumble Badge ) OR AS Mega Bracelet : Southern Island (from Steven after obtaining Latios OR / Latias AS ) S M Key Stone : Ancient Poni Path (from Dexio after becoming Champion ) US UM Key Stone : Poni Plains (from Dexio after becoming Champion ) P E Key Stone : Professor Oak's Laboratory (from Blue after earning seven Badges ) ZA Mega Ring : Jaune Sector 10 (from AZ before facing the Rogue Mega Absol ) In-game appearances [ edit | edit source ] Player characters [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon X and Y , Calem / Serena 's Key Stone is embedded in their black Mega Ring . In Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire , Brendan / May 's Key Stone is embedded in their Mega Bracelet . During Pokémon Contest Spectaculars , Brandon and May instead equip a Mega Cuff and Mega Charm , respectively. In Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon , Elio / Selene 's Key Stone in held in their Z-Ring S M / Z-Power Ring US UM . The Key Stone, however, doesn't appear on the player's model. In Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! , Chase / Elaine doesn't keep their Key Stone in any accessory, and it doesn't appear on the player's model. In Pokémon Legends: Z-A , Paxton / Harmony 's Key Stone is embedded in their golden Mega Ring. Non-player characters [ edit | edit source ] Many non-player characters keep their Key Stones in special items, many of which differ from the items that the player can use. Pokémon X and Y [ edit | edit source ] Korrina uses a Mega Glove (Japanese: メガグローブ Mega Glove ), a white fingerless glove with a red cuff which she wears on her left hand. The Key Stone is set into the back of the glove. Lysandre uses a Mega Ring (Japanese: メガリング Mega Ring ), a silver ring which he wears on his left middle finger. The Key Stone is set into the ring using a bezel. Diantha uses a Mega Charm (Japanese: メガチャーム Mega Charm ), a necklace with a golden spiked ornament. The Key Stone is set into the curved part of the charm. Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire [ edit | edit source ] Steven Stone uses a Mega Stickpin (Japanese: メガラペルピン Mega Lapel Pin ). The Key Stone is set in the stickpin's bezel, which he wears on his jacket's left lapel. Wally uses a Mega Pendant (Japanese: メガペンダント Mega Pendant ). It consists of a silver ornament on a chain, with the Key Stone set in the middle of the ornament. Archie uses a Mega Anchor (Japanese: メガイカリ Mega Anchor ). It consists of a golden anchor chain which he wears around his neck. The Key Stone is set into the anchor's ring. Maxie uses Mega Glasses (Japanese: メガメガネ Mega Glasses ). They are a pair of half-rim glasses with thick black frames, and the Key Stone set into the connection between the frame and the temples on the right side. Lisia uses a Mega Tiara (Japanese: メガティアラ Mega Tiara ). It is a blue hair-clip consisting of three overlapping diamonds, with the Key Stone set into its middle. Zinnia uses a Mega Anklet (Japanese: メガアンクレット Mega Anklet). It is a teal metallic anklet which wraps around her right calf. The Key Stone is set into one of the indentations at the top of the anklet. Courtney and Matt use Mega Rings during the Delta Episode , which are not seen on their artwork. Sidney , Phoebe , Glacia , and Drake use generic Key Stones after the Delta Episode, which are not seen on their artwork. Pokémon Sun, Moon , Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon [ edit | edit source ] Sina and Dexio use white Mega Rings that they each wear around their left wrist. Some opponents at the Battle Tree and Battle Royal Dome use generic Key Stones, which do not show up on their models. Pokémon: Let's Go, Pikachu! and Let's Go, Eevee! [ edit | edit source ] All opponents that can Mega Evolve their Pokémon use generic Key Stones, which do not show up on their models. Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Urbain / Taunie , Naveen , and Lida use golden Mega Rings that they each wear around their left wrist. Emma 's Key Stone is embedded in the chest of her Expansion Suit. Vinnie 's Key Stone is embedded in the palm of his left glove. Tarragon 's Key Stone is embedded in his construction helmet. Canari 's Key Stone is attached to the front zipper on her crop-top. Ivor 's Key Stone is embedded on the back of his right glove. Gwynn 's Key Stone is embedded in her hat. Corbeau and Philippe have their Key Stones set on Rust Syndicate lapel pins, which they wear on their suit jackets. Lebanne 's Key Stone is fixed to the choker which she wears as part of her outfit. Jacinthe 's Key Stone is set on her hairpin. Grisham , Griselle , and L 's Key Stones are embedded in Mega Rings, similar to Lysandre's in Pokémon X and Y. Grisham and Griselle wear them on the middle finger of their left hands, while L keeps it in his pocket. All opponents that can Mega Evolve their Pokémon in the Z-A Royale use generic Key Stones, which do not show up on their models. And the meteoroids fell in their multitude upon a waterfall that had long been home to a tribe of Dragon-type -Pokémon users... [...] The meteors shone with a rainbow brilliance, as if some great life was held within." ( Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire ) ↑ Painting, Lumiose Museum : "The Tower of Mastery stands to the north of Shalour City . Two strange stones were once discovered in this area, which are now thought to have been the first known Mega Stone and Key Stone." ( Pokémon Legends: Z-A )
